title: Replace per-task/epic auto-channels with role-based channels
description:
The chat system auto-creates a channel for every task and epic (chatAutoCreateChannels), causing channel proliferation (15+ channels). Replace with exactly 4 role-based channels: #general, #workers, #architects, #qa.

Changes:
- Remove per-task/epic auto-channel creation entirely (not just disable)
- Add 'role' channel type, remove 'epic' and 'task' types
- Create #workers, #architects, #qa on project init alongside #general
- Add v5→v6 migration that deletes task/epic channels + creates role channels
- Remove chatAutoCreateChannels setting completely
- Update all code referencing getChannelByLinkedEntity, linkedEntityId, slugify
- Update claimNextTask/getContext/completeStep chat hints to use role channels
- Update postSystemMessage to post to #general instead of per-task channels
- Update JetBrains and VS Code channel type definitions
- Remove slugify utility (only used for auto-channel naming)
definitionOfDone: Only 4 channel types exist: general, role, custom
chatAutoCreateChannels setting removed from schema and all code
v5→v6 migration deletes task/epic channels and creates role channels
No code references getChannelByLinkedEntity or linkedEntityId for task/epic linking
claimNextTask chat hint points to role channel not task channel
postSystemMessage posts to #general instead of per-entity channels

implementationPlan.description: Update ChatChannel type in schema.ts (line 287): change type from 'general' | 'epic' | 'task' | 'custom' to 'general' | 'role' | 'custom'. Remove chatAutoCreateChannels from ProjectSettings (line 50). Bump CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION from 5 to 6 (line 64).
implementationPlan.note: Changed ChatChannel.type to 'general' | 'role' | 'custom'. Removed chatAutoCreateChannels from ProjectSettings. Bumped CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION to 6.

implementationPlan.description: Remove auto-channel creation from StateManager.createTask() (lines 1277-1288) and createEpic() (lines 1324-1335). Remove slugify import (line 47). Remove getChannelByLinkedEntity() method (lines 605-609). Update createChannel() validation (line 622) to accept 'general' | 'role' | 'custom'. Remove linkedEntityId duplicate check (lines 632-634) but keep linkedEntityId field as nullable for backward compat. Remove chatAutoCreateChannels reference in sanitizeProject() (line 2234). Update postSystemMessage() (lines 781-789) to post to #general instead of per-entity channel. Remove v4→v5 migration backfill of task/epic channels (lines 2115-2161).
implementationPlan.note: Removed slugify import, getChannelByLinkedEntity(), auto-channel creation in createTask/createEpic, v4→v5 epic/task channel backfill, chatAutoCreateChannels from sanitizeProject. Updated createChannel validTypes to ['general','role','custom']. Changed postSystemMessage to delegate to postToGeneral.

implementationPlan.description: Create role channels on project init. In initProject.ts, after creating #general channel (lines 100-113), create 3 additional channels: #workers (type: 'role'), #architects (type: 'role'), #qa (type: 'role'). Remove chatAutoCreateChannels from default settings (line 86).
implementationPlan.note: Bumped schemaVersion to 6, removed chatAutoCreateChannels, added role channel creation (#workers, #architects, #qa) after #general.

implementationPlan.description: Add v5→v6 migration. Create packages/moe-daemon/src/migrations/v5_to_v6.ts: (1) Delete all channel files with type 'task' or 'epic' from .moe/channels/ and their .moe/messages/ JSONL files, (2) Create #workers, #architects, #qa role channels if they don't already exist, (3) Remove chatAutoCreateChannels from settings, (4) Set schemaVersion to 6. Register in migrations/index.ts. Note: this migration needs filesystem access unlike previous data-only migrations — check existing patterns (v4_to_v5 is data-only; the backfill in StateManager.migrateToV5() handles files). Error handling: wrap each deletion and creation in individual try/catch so partial failures don't abort the migration.
implementationPlan.note: Created v5_to_v6.ts data migration (removes chatAutoCreateChannels). Registered in index.ts. Added migrateToV6FileSystem() to StateManager that deletes task/epic channels and creates role channels with individual try/catch for partial failure resilience.

implementationPlan.description: Update claimNextTask.ts: Remove getChannelByLinkedEntity call (line 149) and task-channel system message posting (lines 152-155). Remove chatCursors auto-set for task channel (lines 158-167). Remove task-channel chat hint (lines 180-187). Instead, find the role channel matching the worker's role (workers/architects/qa) by checking channels where type='role', and set chatHint to point to that channel. Keep generalChannelId logic (already correct).
implementationPlan.note: Replaced getChannelByLinkedEntity + task-channel logic with role-channel lookup by worker role. Posts claim notification to #general. Chat hint points to role channel (#workers/#architects/#qa). Response uses roleChannelId instead of channelId.

implementationPlan.description: Update getContext.ts: Remove getChannelByLinkedEntity call (line 40) and task-channel message fetch (lines 41-51). Instead fetch recent messages from #general channel for context. Update the response to include generalChannelId instead of task channelId.
implementationPlan.note: Replaced getChannelByLinkedEntity with #general channel lookup. Task response now includes generalChannelId instead of channelId. Chat context shows recent #general messages.

implementationPlan.description: Update completeStep.ts: Remove getChannelByLinkedEntity call (line 70) and task-channel chat hint (lines 71-74). Replace with hint pointing to #general for QA handoff notes.
implementationPlan.note: Replaced getChannelByLinkedEntity with #general channel lookup. QA handoff hint now points to #general.

implementationPlan.description: Update chatCreateChannel.ts: Remove linkedEntityId parameter from inputSchema (line 13) and handler params (line 21). Only allow type 'custom' (already does this, just clean up linkedEntityId passthrough on line 33).
implementationPlan.note: Removed linkedEntityId parameter from inputSchema, handler params, and createChannel call.

implementationPlan.description: Delete packages/moe-daemon/src/util/slugify.ts (only used for auto-channel naming in createTask/createEpic/migration backfill — all being removed).
implementationPlan.note: Deleted slugify.ts — no remaining references in codebase.

implementationPlan.description: Update VS Code types: In moe-vscode/src/types/moe.ts (line 89), change ChatChannel.type from 'general' | 'epic' | 'task' | 'custom' to 'general' | 'role' | 'custom'.

Update JetBrains model: In moe-jetbrains/src/main/kotlin/com/moe/model/Models.kt, ChatChannel is already using String type (line 125) so no code change needed — but verify ChatPanel.kt (line 318) still works since it filters by type=='general'. No code change expected but verify.
implementationPlan.note: Verified: JetBrains Models.kt uses String type for ChatChannel.type — no change needed. ChatPanel.kt filters by type=='general' which still works correctly.

implementationPlan.description: Update tests: Fix StateManager.test.ts — remove/update tests referencing auto-channel creation on task/epic create, update getChannelByLinkedEntity tests, add test for new role channel creation on init. Run 'cd packages/moe-daemon && npm run build && npm test' to verify. Error handling: ensure migration test covers partial failure scenarios.
implementationPlan.note: Fixed event emitter test (expects 2 events not 3 since auto-channel creation removed). Removed obsolete findTaskChannel helper and 'sets chatCursors for task channel' test. All 308 tests pass, tsc --noEmit clean.

implementationPlan.description: Update epic rail: The epic-941ca1ca has an epicRail 'Auto-create channels for epics/tasks when chatAutoCreateChannels is true' — update this via moe.update_epic to reflect the new role-based channel approach.
implementationPlan.note: Updated epic rail from 'Auto-create channels for epics/tasks when chatAutoCreateChannels is true' to 'Role-based channels (#general, #workers, #architects, #qa) created on project init — no per-task/epic auto-channels'.
